page: Microsoft Azure DC24s_v3 is a dedicated-core virtual machine belonging to the DCSv3 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated physical cores and 24 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, with a cache configuration of 48 KB L1d, 1 MB L2, and 48 MB L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Performance benchmarks place the DC24s_v3 in the average tier for general compute, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1671.0 and a multi-core score of 15764.0. However, it demonstrates strong capabilities in specific workloads, achieving 15333.0 in Geekbench image processing and a static web serving throughput of 42.52 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is measured at 164.61 GB/sec for large uncached reads and 64.58 GB/sec for large uncached writes. For small-model LLM inference using a 135M model, it processes prompts at 2359.87 tokens/sec and generates text at 306.23 tokens/sec, though performance drops to a weak 2.74 tokens/sec for text generation on large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC24s_v3 offers a high density of memory relative to its 24 dedicated compute cores, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local SSD storage. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs reduces overhead for deployments relying on network-attached premium storage. This configuration is well-suited for memory-optimized applications, database operations, static web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure DC24s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, featuring 24 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 192.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per core ratio. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports premium storage. Benchmarks indicate average general-purpose compute performance, with strong results in image processing, static web serving at 42.52 GB/sec, and small-model LLM inference. This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and web serving applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC24s_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of RAM, and premium storage support, designed for database and memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and high memory density for database and web serving workloads.
